ddls:
CREATE TABLE "polls" (
  "poll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each poll */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the subject of the poll */,
  "modeldate" TEXT /* Model date of the poll */,
  "party" TEXT /* Party affiliation (e.g., all) */,
  "startdate" TEXT /* Start date of the poll */,
  "enddate" TEXT /* End date of the poll */,
  "pollster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the pollster */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade of the pollster (e.g., B/C) */,
  "samplesize" INTEGER /* Sample size of the poll */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population surveyed */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weighting factor for the poll */,
  "influence" REAL /* Influence factor for the poll */,
  "tracking" TEXT /* Tracking information for the poll */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the poll data */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the poll was added to the system */,
  "poll_type" TEXT /* Type of poll (e.g., national, state-level) */,
  "methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used in the poll */,
  "margin_of_error" REAL /* Margin of error for the poll results */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the poll (e.g., active, completed, archived) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the poll data */,
  "geography" TEXT /* Geographical area covered by the poll */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("poll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polls_pollster_id FOREIGN KEY ("pollster_id") REFERENCES pollsters ("pollster_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polls_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subject */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the subject (e.g., economic, healthcare, education) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the subject was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the subject was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pollsters" (
  "pollster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pollster */,
  "pollster_name" TEXT /* Name of the pollster */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade of the pollster (e.g., B/C) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the pollster */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the pollster */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the pollster was founded */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the pollster was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the pollster's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pollster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "poll_id" INTEGER /* ID of the poll the response belongs to */,
  "question" TEXT /* Question being asked */,
  "very" INTEGER /* Number of respondents who answered "very" */,
  "somewhat" INTEGER /* Number of respondents who answered "somewhat" */,
  "not_very" INTEGER /* Number of respondents who answered "not very" */,
  "not_at_all" INTEGER /* Number of respondents who answered "not at all" */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* Text of the response (useful for open-ended questions) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response was last updated */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., multiple choice, open-ended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_poll_id FOREIGN KEY ("poll_id") REFERENCES polls ("poll_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for the subject of the poll
Timestamp of when the poll was added to the system
Margin of error for the poll results
Status of the poll (e.g., active, completed, archived)
Geographical area covered by the poll
Category of the subject (e.g., economic, healthcare, education)
Timestamp of when the subject was created
Timestamp of when the subject was last updated
Timestamp of when the pollster was added to the system
Timestamp of when the pollster's information was last updated
ID of the poll the response belongs to
Number of respondents who answered "not very"
Number of respondents who answered "not at all"
Text of the response (useful for open-ended questions)
Timestamp of when the response was recorded
Timestamp of when the response was last updated
Type of response (e.g., multiple choice, open-ended)
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin)
Timestamp of when the user account was created
Timestamp of when the user account was last updated
Timestamp of the user's last login
Status of the user's account (e.g., active, inactive)
